title: buildah in tekton task deployed via tekton-operator fails on OKD
body:
I am not sure that's the right forum for this bug.  It might well be a configuration issue and thus should be filed in tektoncd/catalog or tektoncd/operator.  But I saw similar bugs here, so.  Please point me to the right issue tracker if that's the wrong one.

Running the `buildah`-ClusterTask I always get the following error.

```
Error: error writing "0 0 4294967295\n" to /proc/30/uid_map: write /proc/30/uid_map: operation not permitted
time="2022-05-12T14:09:11Z" level=error msg="error writing \"0 0 4294967295\\n\" to /proc/30/uid_map: write /proc/30/uid_map: operation not permitted"
time="2022-05-12T14:09:11Z" level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
```

The pod is running as root with the following security config.
```
  securityContext:
    fsGroup: 1001140000
    seLinuxOptions:
      level: s0:c34,c9
  serviceAccount: pipeline
```

comments.body:
@ibotty Does it work if you disable selinux and just make it priviledged ?  I would like you to try securityContext below in both in `Pod` and Tekton `Task` step to just check. But also issue is missing a bit of context is the log shared belongs to the output of Tekton Task running buildah ?

```console
      securityContext:
        privileged: true
```
Is the node running kernel 5.12 or later?  If so, I think you'll need to arrange for the container to have the CAP_SETFCAP capability.
I finally got around to testing with `CAP_SETFCAP` capability.  Yes, that works!  Thank you for your help.  Now I'll have to raise issues to propagate the required capabilities.
